findings.detail: Uses Wilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric) via custom implementation — handles fat-tailed return distributions correctly. Falls back to scipy.stats.ttest_rel in _compute_paired_ttest (not used in primary flow; Wilcoxon is the active test). Paired data: same trading days for both shadows, minimum 5 pairs required, TRIAL_DAYS minimum 21 trading days for statistical power.

Challenger shadows created as shadow_type='challenger' with parent_shadow_id linking to target. get_visible_shadows() excludes challengers (verified in B.0 tests). Opaque to rankings until trial completes. Target shadow cannot see its challenger's analysis — prevents gaming.

3-gate verdict: (1) Wilcoxon p-value < trial_alpha (one-sided, default from settings), (2) Challenger Calmar > calmar_gate (default from settings), (3) Challenger mean > target mean. All three must pass for REPLACE_TARGET. Missing any → RESTORE_TARGET or INCONCLUSIVE. Prevents premature replacement.

get_challenge_validity() uses logistic function on Brier score difference. Well-calibrated challenger vs poorly-calibrated target → validity near 1.0. Poorly-calibrated challenger vs well-calibrated target → validity near 0.0. This prevents miscalibrated shadows from successfully challenging well-calibrated ones.
datetime.now(timezone.utc) used in create_challenger() for timestamp-based ID generation. No bare datetime.now() calls.
